more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Ibhetshu LikaZulu withdraws ConCourt challenge, cites “unrelenting” attacks
more.description: Civic organisation Ibhetshu LikaZulu has withdrawn its Constitutional Court challenge against the government’s proposed Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment (No. 3) Bill, which is linked to the controversial 2030 agenda seeking to extend President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s tenure.The post Ibhetshu LikaZulu withdraws ConCourt challenge, cites “unrelenting” attacks appeared first on Nehanda Radio.

more.title: Lawyers Keep USD450 000 Legal Fees
more.description: Source: Lawyers Keep USD450 000 Legal Fees Lawyers involved in Matabeleland pressure group Ibhetshu LikaZulu and secretary-general Mbuso Fuzwayo’s Constitutional Court bid to block President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s term extension will keep their staggering legal fees involving US$450 000 despite their client withdrawing the case in a shock move this afternoon, an insider said. more.description: Old Mutual Senior Manager Jailed 15 Years For Raping Scholarship Student On Attachment A high-flying Old Mutual senior manager has been caged for 15 years after a court found him guilty of two counts of rape, targeting a vulnerable university student who was not only on attachment at the company but also a beneficiary of [...]The post 15 YEARS: Old Mutual Senior Manager Jailed For Raping Scholarship Student appeared first on iHarare News.

more.title: Fuzwayo Withdraws Court Challenge Against ZANU PF’s 2030 Agenda
more.description: Bulawayo-based political activist Mbuso Fuzwayo and his pressure group, Ibhetshu Likazulu, have withdrawn their Constitutional Court challenge against the proposed Constitutional Amendment Number 3, which seeks to extend President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s term beyond 2028. The withdrawal takes effect immediately.

more.title: Zimbabwe Vigil Diary 7th March 2026
more.description: Once again Vigil activists met outside the Zimbabwe Embassy in London to continue our protest against the human rights abuse and lack of democracy in Zimbabwe perpetrated by ZANU PF, the ruling regime.

more.title: Zimbabwe’s Shocking Social Media Ban: Are They Protecting Children or Silencing a Generation’s Voice Online?
more.description: HARARE — The Zimbabwean government is currently drafting a controversial new policy that could see children under the age of 18 banned from using social media platforms. more.title: Zimbabwe lacks capacity to detect 17 rare earth minerals in lithium exports at its borders
more.description: Zimbabwe currently does not have the technology at its borders to detect the full range of rare earth minerals that may be contained within lithium or other mineral exports, the Minister of Mines and Mining Development, Polite Kambamura, has told Parliament.The post Zimbabwe lacks capacity to detect 17 rare earth minerals in lithium exports at its borders appeared first on Nehanda Radio.

more.description: PACE bowler Blessing Muzarabani has been named in the recently ended T20 Team of the Tournament. The announcement was made by the International Cricket Council (ICC), following India’s successful title defence against New Zealand, naming Muzarabani in the team of the tournament alongside global icons like Jasprit Bumrah and Sanju Samson.
